mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that absorbs rate hikes, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets price in soft landing scenarios.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of tightening periods, or the mechanism shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth correlates with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand, supporting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% signals strong aggregate demand that translates into corporate earnings upgrades, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in > 60% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 3.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding economic output and higher corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below random chance in out-of-sample tests.
